pii_fields.status: CONFIRMED via code — AUTH_KEY and JWT_SECRET_KEY are hardcoded placeholder strings ('...OfDoomThatMustBeAMinimumNumberOfBytes'), and DEFAULT_PASSWORD = "Pass@word1" is used for seeded demo accounts. Both have // TODO comments acknowledging they should not be used in production.

summary.highest_sensitivity_concern: Hardcoded secrets/credentials in AuthorizationConstants.cs (AUTH_KEY, JWT_SECRET_KEY, DEFAULT_PASSWORD = 'Pass@word1') — PaymentMethod entity is now CONFIRMED not persisted and is no longer a concern.
summary.recommendation: Confirm hardcoded secrets in AuthorizationConstants.cs are dev/demo-only and overridden via environment/config in real deployments before any data migration that touches IdentityDb.
